name: Rin Rin
classification_notes: Described explicitly as Transitional period, reviving Old Style No. 1 by Alexander Phemister; characterised by near-vertical stress, strong thick-thin contrast and horizontal head serifs — hallmarks of the transitional classification.
summary: A revival of Alexander Phemister's Old Style No. 1, expanded into five weights with italics for contemporary print and digital use.
description: Designed by Cristian Vargas at Typozon, this revival traces back to Phemister's 1829 design produced at Miller and Richards in Edinburgh. The original single cut has been extended into five weights from Light to Bold, each with a matching italic, to suit a broader range of typographic contexts. Latin coverage spans Basic Latin, Latin-1 Supplement and Latin Extended-A; the typeface is expected to release in July 2026.
foundry_blurb: Typozon describe Rin Rin as a revival of Old Style No. 1, a Transitional period typeface originally designed by Alexander Phemister in 1829 at Miller and Richards in Edinburgh. The project aims to expand the original design with additional weights and styles to improve versatility across both print and digital formats, with desktop, web, app and logo licences planned.

name: Salvaje Display
classification_notes: A boldly expressive display typeface with distinctive letterform alternates and a strong visual character suited to headlines and large-scale applications; the italic adds further personality, placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A two-style display family by Cristian Vargas, inspired by the elaborate plumage and courtship rituals of Birds of Paradise, recognised by the Type Directors Club and Typographica in 2018.
description: Designed by Cristian Vargas at Typozon, Salvaje Display draws its visual logic from the Birds of Paradise, translating those creatures' extreme ornamentation into letterforms with strong presence and a rich set of OpenType alternates. The family comprises Regular and Italic, with alternate glyphs for A, E, y, Y and the ampersand alongside discretionary ligatures and case-sensitive forms. Originally released in 2018 and updated to v2.0 in 2025, it has been applied to consumer product identities, album artwork, book covers and editorial projects.
foundry_blurb: Typozon describe Salvaje Display as a display family derived from the Latin silvaticus, meaning 'wild' or 'of the woods', inspired by the Birds of Paradise of New Guinea and Australia. They note the birds' evolution of extreme visual display in the absence of natural predators, and position the typeface as sharing that same capacity for bold, attention-commanding beauty across print and digital contexts including posters, packaging, magazines and environmental graphics.

name: Chosmos
classification_notes: A chunky, organic display typeface with no serifs and strongly expressive, curved counterforms. The three angled styles (Backslant, Regular, Slant) position it firmly as a display type with no body-text intent.
summary: A three-style display family with chunky, organic forms available in Regular, Slant and Backslant cuts, designed for headlines and bold identity work.
description: Designed by Cristian Vargas at Typozon and released in April 2025, Chosmos pairs heavy weight with curvy counterforms to offset its impact. The three styles — Backslant, Regular and Slant — provide directional contrast for dynamic typographic layouts, particularly effective with tight or negative tracking. It targets branding, packaging, editorial, sports and music contexts where a strong, rhythmic presence is required.
foundry_blurb: Typozon describe Chosmos as a display family born from chaos yet shaped with intent, drawing on explosive energy and the fluid aftermath of motion. Three styles — Backslant, Regular and Slant — add movement, dimension and contrast, while curvy counterforms soften its weighty presence. The foundry position it for headlines, branding, packaging, editorial, sports, action, CPG and music-related work, noting that negative tracking unlocks striking and unconventional layout possibilities.

name: Lust Dotta
classification_notes: No page content was available; classified as display based on the name conventions typical of decorative type releases, but this is speculative.
summary: No public page content was available for this typeface at the time of cataloguing.
description: The page for Lust Dotta at Typozon was password-protected and returned no accessible content. Classification, weights, and other details could not be determined from the available information. This entry should be revisited once the page becomes publicly accessible.
foundry_blurb: No foundry copy was available as the page is password-protected.

name: Salvaje Text
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a display typeface family; its inspiration in the ornate visual drama of Birds of Paradise suggests a decorative, expressive character suited to headline and editorial use rather than body text.
summary: An unreleased display family by Cristian Vargas, drawn from the visual extravagance of Birds of Paradise native to New Guinea and Australia.
description: Salvaje Text takes its name from the Latin silvaticus, meaning wild or of the woods, and draws its formal inspiration from the elaborate plumage and shape-shifting courtship displays of Birds of Paradise. The family is currently in development with weights and styles yet to be confirmed, though planned licensing covers desktop, web, app and logo use. Latin coverage across Basic Latin, Latin-1 Supplement and Latin Extended-A is intended for editorial, packaging, book covers and digital applications.
foundry_blurb: Typozon describe Salvaje Text as a display typeface family designed to capture attention with subtle beauty and clarity, suited to editorial pieces, book covers, magazines, packaging, websites and apps. The project is currently in progress, with weights, styles and release date still to be confirmed.

name: Gumption Caps
classification_notes: An all-caps custom display typeface designed for brand identity use, with no lowercase and a single weight, placing it firmly in the display category rather than a general-purpose serif or sans-serif.
summary: A single-weight all-caps display typeface commissioned by Lippincott for Gumption Coffee's brand identity, designed by Cristian Vargas at Typozon.
description: Designed by Cristian Vargas as a bespoke commission for Gumption Coffee, an Australian specialty coffee brand expanding across the United States, the typeface was developed in partnership with Lippincott as part of a broader visual identity system. It exists in one weight, caps only, with Basic Latin and Latin-1 coverage. The identity work was recognised by Underconsideration, and the typeface licence remains exclusive to Gumption Coffee until October 2025.
foundry_blurb: Typozon partnered with Lippincott to create a custom typeface for Gumption Coffee, an award-winning Sydney-based specialty coffee brand that opened its first US location in Brooklyn in 2020 and has since expanded into Manhattan. The typeface forms part of a new brand identity system and was engineered by Ramiro Espinoza at Re:Type.
